finding_results.notes: Compressed the Malfoy exchange from a nine-line back-and-forth into a three-line practical check ('Malfoy all right to work with?' / 'He's helpful, actually.' / 'Good.'). I know what that house is') with a blunter sequence: he asserts she's not sleeping, interrupts her defense, and states two plain observations ('You look knackered, and you're living in a house that moves its own walls. That's what I'm asking about.'). Harry's pressure now stays anchored to Hermione's exhaustion and the house rather than pivoting into a separate Malfoy evaluation. The sleeve-adjustment beat (line 667) and Hermione's lie (line 669) are preserved unchanged.

The snapshot's standalone 'Mm.' (Draco's style-bible example line) had already been replaced with 'What.' at line 379 in the current chapter. The snapshot's standalone 'Yeah.' (Harry's style-bible example line) had already been replaced with 'Yes.' at line 579. A full-text search confirmed no style-bible example line appears verbatim or near-verbatim anywhere in the chapter. The only 'Yeah' occurrence is 'Yeah, you do.' at line 519, which is a full sentence response, not a standalone acknowledgment matching the example line.

summary: Two findings addressed. The Harry pub scene was revised to eliminate the composed multi-clause speech and reduce the Malfoy interrogation to a brief practical probe, keeping Harry's pressure focused on Hermione's exhaustion and the house per the boundary context pack. Style-bible example-line reuse was already resolved in the current chapter text; no verbatim or near-verbatim matches remain. All must-land beats preserved: library research scenes, Mark resonance onset, Harry friendship scene with lie, dual sleeve adjustments, and the mark_resonance_onset setup. Heading contract intact.
